other_observers: Nick Komar; I was the finder of the bird, no one else saw it after us.
description: I first noticed the bird by the thick black M on the back of the bird. The underparts were white other than a small black wingtip. The back was gray. The primaries and secondaries were white. It had a white tail with a black bar at the end of the tail. Then at a closer inspection it had a black bill and black legs. It also had a black bar on the back of the neck and a black ear spot. It was just a little smaller than the nearby Ring-billed Gulls.Behaviors: It was flying around the reservoir occasionally landing on the water for up to 20 seconds before taking off again.Call: Didn’t hear itPlumage: Juvenile
